title: Surround (the Plugin That Should Be Built In)
subtitle: {key:ds}, {key:cs}, {key:ys} — operate on surroundings as a first-class concept.
summary: Tim Pope's vim-surround adds three operators: ys (add surrounding), ds (delete surrounding), cs (change surrounding). Once you have it, it feels like core Vim.

blocks.text: Tim Pope's [vim-surround](https://github.com/tpope/vim-surround) is the plugin most often cited as "this should ship with Vim." It adds three operators that treat *surroundings* (quotes, brackets, tags) as first-class targets.

rows: Change surrounding tag (prompts for new tag name)
Surround inner word with single quotes

blocks.title: Why It Belongs in Core
blocks.text:
Vim's grammar already understands the *content* inside surroundings — `i"`, `i(`, `it`, etc. select the inner content. Surround completes the grammar by treating the *delimiters themselves* as a first-class concept that can be deleted, changed, or added.

The operator names are mnemonic: **d**elete-**s**urrounding, **c**hange-**s**urrounding, **y**ank-**s**urrounding ("surround with"). The plugin makes surroundings as natural to manipulate as words or lines — which is why most Vim users add it day one.

blocks.title: Where You Already Have It
blocks.text:
Surround is so universally adopted that it ships baked into several Vim emulations:

  - **VS Code Vim extension** (vscodevim/Vim) — surround commands ({key:y}{key:s}, {key:c}{key:s}, {key:d}{key:s}) work out of the box, no separate plugin needed. Enable with `"vim.surround": true` (default on).
  - **IdeaVim** (JetBrains IDEs) — bundled as the IdeaVim-Surround plugin, one click to install.
  - **Vim** and **Neovim** (the actual editors) — *not* built in. You still need a plugin. The two living maintained options are [`tpope/vim-surround`](https://github.com/tpope/vim-surround) (the original, works in both) and [`kylechui/nvim-surround`](https://github.com/kylechui/nvim-surround) (Lua rewrite for Neovim, slightly different defaults — see Strategy notes in the codebase). Some users prefer [`echasnovski/mini.surround`](https://github.com/echasnovski/mini.surround) as part of the `mini.nvim` collection.

Neovim has discussed adopting a built-in surround module several times but as of Neovim 0.10/0.11 it remains a plugin choice.
